front: Work: performance in which the artist pulled a scroll from her vagina and read from it
Work: scroll text describes meeting a "structuralist filmmaker" who said "we can be friends equally tho we are not artists equally"
Work: orgy-like performance in which eight performers ecstatically rolled around with raw fish, chicken, and sausages
Work: silent film documenting the artist having sex with partner James Tenney while her cat Kitch looks on
Work: grid of mostly out-of-focus Polaroids documenting the artist's morning ritual of kissing her cats
Work: book born from the artist's misunderstanding that Cézanne was a woman; read aloud at the start of Interior Scroll
back: Cezanne, She Was a Great Painter (book) (Carolee Schneemann)
work: Cezanne, She Was a Great Painter (book)

front: Work: 36 "transformative actions" in which the artist used her nude body as part of an assemblage; inspired by Cretan snake goddesses
Artist: American feminist performance artist; suspended from Bard College for painting a nude self-portrait
Artist: performance artist who drew on walls while strapped in a harness in Up to and Including Her Limits; responded to MacArthur Foundation with "I do not have health insurance"
